Scroll

About

Polymath
from the
Niger Delta

Ikanke-Abasi Umana is a programs and operations professional, researcher, writer, and event host based in Akwa Ibom State, Nigeria. She holds a B.A. in Communication Arts from the University of Uyo, graduating as Best Student of the 2019/2020 session with a 4.14 GPA — the highest in her class.

She currently serves as Consulting Analyst at Mosaic Human Capital Innovations (MHCI), where she has built entire operational systems — from talent placement ventures with full legal frameworks, to women-in-tech programmes coordinated across government, AfriLabs, and international partners. She is also Co-Founder of Founder Advocacy Network (FAN).

She has been moderated panels, hosted events, and served on judging panels since she was a teenager — from NISSMUN in 2018 to Akwa Ibom Tech Week 2025. Her work spans the full arc from field research to flagship events, always returning to the same conviction: that clarity and structure are acts of care.

4+ Years building programs
and running operations
2 Academic research papers
(one forthcoming in publication)
12+ Events hosted or
produced end-to-end
3yr On Inspiration FM as
radio host and presenter

The Work

Four rooms,
one address.

01

Programs & Operations

Built TalentWorks (MHCI) from scratch — legal frameworks, candidate dossiers, placements. Architected TechRise Women Initiative for 250-participant Cohort 1 with GIFA and AfriLabs. Managed HOME Project (Triift Africa × iSafe Hub, Ogun State). Co-founded Founder Advocacy Network. Delivered Akwa Ibom Tech Week 2025 end-to-end.

MHCITalentWorksTWITriift AfricaFAN
02

Research & Analysis

Forthcoming publication: seminar work on gender representation in UNICEF's X posts — how stories about boys were systematically underrepresented. Undergraduate thesis on newspaper coverage of Nigeria's fuel subsidy removal (ThisDay and The Punch, 79-edition sample). Writes analytical memos connecting macro data to organisational strategy.

Gender StudiesMedia AnalysisPolicy Research
03

Writing & Voice

Founder of On This Note — essays on living, culture, and the particular texture of being Nigerian. Essays reviewed at Jollof & Fiction; invited to speak on Pluralism. Companion audio series VoiceNotes on Spotify. Three years hosting Teens' Time with Chichi on Inspiration FM 105.9. Voice-over work for Victory Chapel and Word of Grace Bible Church.

SubstackVoiceNotes PodcastRadio
04

Events & Hosting

Patron and programme lead for Founders Friday Uyo — monthly editions since April 2025, including LGA tours (Eket, IK). Judge at WTM Uyo IWD 2026. Hosted Engineering Night (UniUyo, 2023), Church Clothes runway (2024 & 2026), Gatsby red carpet (2025). Participated in US DoS IVLP Journalism Masterclass with Aniekeme Finbarr. Designs all Founders Friday Uyo promotional materials.

Founders FridayAKTW 2025Church ClothesWTM

2025

Akwa Ibom Tech Week

MHCI / GIFA · Ibom Innovation Network

End-to-end delivery lead. Designed session lineup, Run of Show, and hosted GIFA First Lady's Breakfast for Women in Tech — the first of its kind. Moderated fireside with Defcomm CEO. Managed Innovation Challenge Semi-finals.

The Enablers Network

Patron and programme lead for the Uyo chapter. Monthly editions since April 2025, including LGA tours to Eket and IK. Designed the 5-pillar growth framework, curates sessions, coordinates stakeholders, and designed all promotional materials — from launch stickers to event flyers.

2021 — 2024

Teens' Time with Chichi

9ineteen Network · Inspiration FM 105.9

Hosted a weekly youth radio show for three years — live interviews, curated discussions, and music. Launched the show's Spotify account and managed its Instagram. Also featured in a French-language segment on AKBC during her PR internship.

2024

IVLP Journalism Masterclass

US Dept. of State · Aniekeme Finbarr

Participated in a US Department of State IVLP Awardee-led journalism masterclass on ethical reporting and AI, training 60 journalism graduates across Nigeria.

On This Note

Essays on culture, faith, womanhood, friendship, and the particular texture of being Nigerian and alive. Influenced by Hasan Minhaj's precision, Zikoko's warmth, and The Big Daily's wit.

On This Note

Research

Work that
asks harder
questions.

Forthcoming publication

Gender Representation in UNICEF's Posts on X: A Content Analysis

Seminar research examining how a major international organisation presents childhood through gendered lenses on social media. The study found stories centring boys were systematically underrepresented — challenging assumptions about whose vulnerability gets amplified online.

Postgraduate Seminar · Communication Studies · University of Uyo
Undergraduate thesis · 2024

Coverage of Nigeria's Fuel Subsidy Removal by Select Nigerian Newspapers

Content analysis of 79 editions each from ThisDay and The Punch. Found initial high frequency declining over six months, with framing favouring economic impacts and government perspectives over public voice. Recommended consistent coverage and diverse viewpoints.

B.A. Communication Arts · University of Uyo · CGPA 4.14/5 · Best Graduating Student
